Vivek Prakash (OISD)

Scope
This document lays down the minimum requirements of layout within the 
plant boundary/ pipeline installations for all midstream, downstream and 
upstream (except offshore installations) sectors of the Oil and Gas Industry. 
The requirement of green belt/ buffer zone beyond the plant boundary is 
outside the scope of this standard. Such provisions should be considered 
based on local environmental/ security requirements/ requirements of 
applicable statutory bodies. 
This standard does not apply in petroleum installations and upstream 
facilities with aggregate storage capacity of less than 1000 KL. At such 
locations inter distances shall be as per risk assessment.

Non Compliance 
If an entity has laid, built, constructed, expanded the Installation based on 
some other standard or is not meeting the requirements specified in this 
standard, the entity shall carry out a detailed Quantitative Risk Analysis 
(QRA) of its infrastructure and the entity shall thereafter take approval from 
its highest decision-making body or its board for non-conformities and 
mitigation measures. The entity’s Board approval along with the compliance 
report, gap analysis, mitigation measures and implementation schedule 
shall be submitted to the OISD.

Layout of Block/ Facilities
• Units in Single Block
• Control Room Location
• Overhead power transmission lines shall not pass over the installation
• Cooling Tower Location
• In undulating terrain, process units should be located at higher elevation as 
compared to storage tanks 
• Flare should be located upwind of process units and hydrocarbon storage. The area 
around flare should be paved. 
• Smoking shall not be permitted inside the installation. 
• Oily water sewer (OWS)/ Contaminated rain water sewer (CRWS) pit (open type)/ oil 
catcher shall be located at a distance not less than 30 m from fired heater/ any fired 
equipment. If the OWS/ CRWS pit is covered with vent to safe location, the 
minimum separation distance shall be 15m.

Inter distances Between Block/ Facilities
✓ Table-1 shall be 
referred.
✓ There are 
 24 types 
of facilities considered.
✓ In this revision few new 
type of facilities added  
(LNG Tanks, Excluded 
petroleum storage, 
Utilities plants, DWST 
tanks, etc.)
Highlights:
• Total 29 notes to table.
• Sterile Radius of flare can be reduced up to 
60M (refer N7), based on thermal radiation 
calculation.
“Any tank, distillation column, pump-house or any facility for 
the refining, cracking, reforming, blending, storage or handling 
of petroleum or liquefied petroleum gas, other than knockout 
drum and condensate recovery pump attached to flare, shall be 
located at least 90 M away from flare. No manned permanent 
structure/ shelter / storage of flammable materials/ open drains 
shall be allowed in the sterile radius for flare. Suitable thermal 
radiation study for the flare as per API-521 shall be done for 
determining the actual sterile subject to conforming the 
following conditions”

Inter distances Between Block/ Facilities
• Fire water storage (other than steel tank) can be located within 90 M radius from flare, 
provided the same is outside the impact zone of fire fireball.
• A captive power plant is considered a process unit for inter-distance consideration.
• Inter distances covered related to polymer warehouses, excluded petroleum storage etc.
• Revised OISD-STD-118 consists 12 inter-distance tables, which covers all kind of facilities. 
Separate tables are given for process plants, tank farms, POL location, LPG marketing 
Locations, Upstream sector and pipeline locations. 
• These inter-distance tables are consistent with tables given in various other standards
(OISD-STD-144, 244, 141, etc.).

Layout of Process Units
• While planning unit layout Safety, Operation, Maintenance ease shall be considered.
• No trenches, pits (open/ closed) and open drain funnel shall be located within 15 Meters 
from furnace walls. 
• Air fin coolers should be installed above the pipe rack/ technological structures/ 
independent structure. Pumps handling hydrocarbons above the temperature of 230 °C 
or C4 and Lighter ends should not be installed underneath the air fin coolers. If 
unavoidable, then blind floor shall be provided beneath the air fin cooler. 
• Table-2 shall be followed for inter-distances of equipment inside process unit.

LAYOUT OF STORAGE TANKS 
• Dyke Enclosure Capacity
✓ 120000 m³ for Floating Roof Tank
✓ 60000 m³ for Fixed Roof Tank
• Fixed cum floating roof tanks shall be treated as fixed roof tanks.
• If a group of tanks contain both fixed and floating roof tanks, then it shall be treated as 
a group of fixed roof tanks for the purpose of above limits.
• In case of Earthen Dyke “.6M top flat section shall be provided with stone/ PCC/ Brick 
pitching on side slopes and top of dyke”. 
• Dyke height more than 2M is allowed with approval from licensing authority with 
following special arrangements:

LAYOUT OF STORAGE TANKS (Cont.) 
✓ Monitors on raised platforms (outside the dyke), if required, shall be provided so that 
the trajectory of the monitors is not restricted. 
✓ All the tanks inside such dyke shall be provided with water spray system, irrespective of 
the tank diameter. 
✓ Tank farm area shall be covered through CCTV surveillance system and the same shall 
be continuously monitored. 
✓ Suitable arrangements shall be made all around the tank farm such that the full tank 
height is visible from outside dyke.
• Dyke wall shall be an independent structure and no other structure shall be provide/ 
supported on dyke wall. 
Table 3,4,5 shall be referred for the Safety Inter Distance of 
tankfarm

Grouping of Storage Tanks
• Grouping of Class A, B, and C petroleum
• Storage of Excluded class petroleum
LAYOUT OF C4 & LIGHTER ENDS (e.g. LPG, Propane, Butane, 
Propylene etc.) FACILITIES
• This is same as stipulations for LPG facilities in previous revision, Other products 
have been added.
• Table-6 & Table-7. shall be used for Safety Inter distances
